# Engine to generate protected event Settlements for Bidding

## Usage

See `.buildkite/prepare-bid-distribution.yml` for the production pipeline.

### 1. Gathering input data

```bash
epoch=980
bucket=marinade-validator-bonds-mainnet
pipeline_dir="/tmp/bond-pipeline-${epoch}" && mkdir -p "$pipeline_dir"

# generated by @marinade-finance/validator-bonds/snapshot-parser-cli
gcloud storage cp "gs://$bucket/$epoch/stakes.json" "${pipeline_dir}/stakes.json"

# validators data (past-validators.json may not exist for the epoch)
gcloud storage cp "gs://$bucket/$epoch/validators.json" "${pipeline_dir}/validators.json"
gcloud storage cp "gs://$bucket/$epoch/past-validators.json" "${pipeline_dir}/past-validators.json" || true

# SAM auction results from the ds-sam-pipeline GitHub repo: pick the highest
# <slot> directory for the epoch under auctions/<epoch>.<slot>/
curl -o "${pipeline_dir}/results.json" \
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/marinade-finance/ds-sam-pipeline/main/auctions/${epoch}.<slot>/outputs/results.json"

# evaluation.json: produced by ds-sam `analyze-revenues` over the auction
# inputs/outputs and validators.json (see the "DS SAM generates PMPE bids data"
# step in the buildkite pipeline), or fetch the pre-generated copy from GCS:
gcloud storage cp "gs://$bucket/$epoch/bid-psr-distribution-evaluation.json" "${pipeline_dir}/evaluation.json"

# SSI/SSR pmpe for the scoring epoch is fetched by the CLI from apy-api at
# GET <apy-api>/v1/epoch-pmpe/ssr — the pipeline cannot run an epoch until apy-api
# has SSI/SSR for that epoch. Configure via --apy-api-url or APY_API_URL
# (default: https://apy.marinade.finance).

# Rewards input data from stakes-etl
rewards_dir="${pipeline_dir}/rewards"
mkdir -p "$rewards_dir"
gs_bucket_etl='gs://marinade-stakes-etl-mainnet'
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_mev.json" "$rewards_dir"/mev.json
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_validators_mev.json" "$rewards_dir"/validators_mev.json
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_inflation.json" "$rewards_dir"/inflation.json
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_validators_inflation.json" "$rewards_dir"/validators_inflation.json
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_validators_blocks.json" "$rewards_dir"/validators_blocks.json
gcloud storage cp "$gs_bucket_etl/$epoch/rewards_priority_fee.json" "$rewards_dir"/jito_priority_fee.json
```

### 2. Generating Settlements

Fee parameters and per-settlement configuration live in the repo's
`settlement-config.yaml`.

```bash
cargo run --release --bin bid-distribution-cli -- \
    --settlement-config settlement-config.yaml \
    --stake-meta-collection "${pipeline_dir}/stakes.json" \
    --sam-results-collection "${pipeline_dir}/results.json" \
    --rewards-dir "$rewards_dir" \
    --validator-meta-collection "${pipeline_dir}/validators.json" \
    --revenue-expectation-collection "${pipeline_dir}/evaluation.json" \
    --output-settlement-collection "${pipeline_dir}/bid-distribution-settlements.json" \
    --output-protected-event-collection "${pipeline_dir}/protected-events.json"
```

`--sam-meta-collection` (the scoring API `/scores/sam?epoch=N` shape) is a
mutually-exclusive alternative to `--sam-results-collection`, kept for tooling
(`scripts/regression-test-settlements.sh`, `scripts/simulate-fee.ts`).

Merkle trees are generated from the settlement collection by the separate
`merkle-generator-cli` (see `settlement-distributions/merkle-generator`).

## Settlement claim kinds

Every claim in the output settlement JSON carries a `kind` discriminator:

- `StakerPayout` — a payout to a staker; carries `active_stake`, `activating_stake`
  and `stake_accounts` (the staker's stake accounts the settlement applies to).
- `FeeDeposit` — the Marinade/DAO fee cut; carries only the authority pair and
  `claim_amount`. It intentionally has NO `stake_accounts`: the fee-deposit
  account is the _destination_ of the transfer, not the subject of the
  settlement, so listing it under `stake_accounts` was misleading (it shadowed
  the staker-payout semantics).
- `Marker` — zero-amount technical claim distinguishing otherwise-identical
  merkle roots; no stake data.

A consumer that needs the fee-deposit account(s) for a `FeeDeposit` claim can
reconstruct them from the epoch's `stakes.json`: select the stake metas whose
`(withdraw_authority, stake_authority)` equals the claim's authority pair (the
Marinade/DAO fee authorities configured in `settlement-config.yaml`) and project
`pubkey` → `active_delegation_lamports`.
